title: panicum capillarioides
intro: Panicum capillarioides, also known as African rice or East Indian rice grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Panicum capillarioides has been used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The plant's medicinal properties also make it beneficial for digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, and it has been used to treat respiratory problems like bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, decoction, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
